title: Add terminology glossary to documentation for Wheelwright framework terms
perceive: External documentation (README) uses 'Track' clearly but internal docs (KnowMe, CLAUDE) introduce undefined terms (lug, spoke, wheel, teaching, signal, epic) without glossary. New users bounce between frameworks without vocabulary bridge.
execute: 1. Add 'Terminology' or 'Glossary' section to README.md after 'Why This Matters' section.
2. Include one-sentence definitions for core Wheelwright terms visible in Tracks documentation:
- Lug: Work unit describing a task, feature, bug, or signal within the framework
- Spoke: A project or service within the Wheelwright fleet with autonomous advisory system
- Wheel: A spoke instance with its own WAI-Spoke directory and state
- Signal: Cross-spoke notification of patterns, patterns, or framework-level observations
- Teaching: Packaged framework update distributed from hub to all wheels
3. Add pointer to AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md for deeper technical explanation.
4. Keep glossary concise (100-150 words total) for readability.
verify: Glossary section appears in README between 'Why This Matters' and 'Three Ways', all 6+ terms defined clearly, no cross-reference errors.
acceptance_criteria: Glossary added to README, all key terms from KnowMe/CLAUDE defined, links to detailed docs present.
completion_note: Terminology/Glossary section added to tracks/README.md after 'Why This Matters' section. All 6 core terms (Track, Lug, Spoke, Wheel, Signal, Teaching) defined with clear one-sentence descriptions (~120 words total). Added pointer to AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md for deeper technical explanation. Section positioned correctly between 'Why This Matters' and 'Three Ways to Generate a Track'.
